raw_input:
[5.0/5] ... then what i wanted but the razor was very nice. The blades are a little thinner then what i wanted but the razor was very nice. [5.0/5] Five Stars No more overpriced disposable razors for me anymore. [5.0/5] Simply Outstanding! As an experienced wet shaver for many years, I have an extensive collection of razors ranging from very inexpensive to those costing hundreds of dollars.
I bought this ACEVIVI, as a friend recommended it. I really didn't expect much at this price point and thought if nothing else I would reuse the handle. What a pleasant surprise this razor provided.
The fit and finish of the razor is near perfect with beautiful heavy chrome plating. The head assembly is aluminum and the handle is heavy chromed steel, but the overall balance is still excellent. The 4" long handle is excellent for those who prefer long handles or who have large hands as I do. The deep but comfortable knurling on the handle is some of the best I've seen, providing an excellent grip.
The blade alignment is excellent with equal blade exposure on both sides using different brands of blades.
The razor comes with five ACEVIVI blades (which I have not yet used) in a very nice black presentation box with a heavy black high density foam insert suggesting a much more expensive razor.
The shave characteristics of this razor are excellent. One side is open comb and the other is closed comb representing the best of both worlds. I didn't note a significant difference in aggressiveness between the two sides. The razor has significant blade exposure and blade gap and is definitely an aggressive razor providing a very close shave. In spite of the blade exposure and gap, it provided an exceptionally smooth and comfortable shave.
Overall, this razor is an exceptional buy and out-shaves most high end razors costing many times more. With proper care, this razor should last a lifetime.
I would definitely recommend this razor to anyone interested in traditional wet shaving. [2.0/5] Inefficient Razor that's Rough to Use: There are much better value bargain razors out there. The Acevivi Razor : A Test and Review
I only came across this razor very recently. It intrigued me because some seemed to like it and report that it was well made. Also, like the new Fendrihan razors, it has an unusual head: Closed comb on one side; open comb on the other. I think the idea is that it offers two levels of aggression. The Lutz slant offers a similar choice of two levels of aggression.

In use I found that both sides offered about the same level of aggression. Another reason for the test is that it's a low cost razor; under $13 currently on Amazon. I'm always intrigued by potential bargains!

A further unusual feature is the exceptionally long handle. Longer than the Futur, longer than my Mergress, Schick Krona, or even my long handled Gillette Starburst, (aimed at the ladies” market); longer in fact than any razor I own. To some this is a plus, to others not so. It may be useful if ladies wish to use the razor on their legs, or other parts. The razor is well balanced so the long handle presented no problem.

The razor comes with a box of five blades. I have no idea of their origin. But since the razor is Chinese, I expect China.

The Blade used for the Test
I chose to use the Rapira Platinum Lux. I've found this blade to be consistent and reliable in just about every razor (but there are some razors where it's an indifferent pairing, say with my RR Wunderbar), and it's always good for at least four shaves, often six, in just about any razor. It's sharp, smooth, comfortable, very cheap to buy, and easily available, and my personal favorite of the Rapira line. I've also used it every DE razor I own at one time or another, and I own over 50 DE safety razors . Not my favorite blade, but a very good one in most razors. I used a brand new blade

How I Used this Razor for the Test
When using an adjustable razor, (and following advice from others on this forum, including Jim Chandler, plus my own long experimentation) I've found the best way, for the smoothest and most comfortable shave is to INCREASE the aggression with each pass. It seems counter-intuitive at first but it really works best. It's like lowering the blade on a lawn mower to get a closer cut. I increased the aggression on each pass on this test.

Since this razor supposedly offered two choices of aggression I used the Acevivi in this way: Less aggressive WTG and XTG, then more aggressive ATG and for buffing. I'm assuming that closed comb is less aggressive, though as some have reported with the Fendrihan, this is not always the case.

Now in previous reviews, I notice that some people seem to miss this point. I really believe that this is the best way to get a very smooth, comfortable, and importantly irritation-free shave with an adjustable razor. I have a complete thread on this method. Please review the thread and give it a whirl, and report what you find please!

The Routine
For testing I go through the same routine. I clean my face and wet the beard by washing with a glycerin soap. I rinse off the soap, wet it thoroughly and apply two drops of a pre-shave oil.

By the way, I believe good pre-shave prep not only produces a better shave, but reduces irritation, and keeps you skin in good condition. I know of one rather famous reviewer whose skin frankly looks irritated, red, blotchy and awful, especially on his neck. I don't doubt he gets close shaves, but at what cost?

I use an Arko shave stick applied directly to the beard and lather with a Stirling Synthetic “Kong” brush. I rinse and lather again between passes. I thoroughly rinse the head of the razor between passes.

I remove the blade from the razor when I'm done. I dry it with a microfiber cloth. Naturally I dry the razor too when I'm done.

I finish with an Alum block, which is a good indicator of razor burn or irritation. Then after washing it off , I apply Thayer's Alcohol Free Witch Hazel.

The Shave
I shaved 22 hours before doing a test comparison of the Parker Variant and Ming Shi 2000s, so my face had a little less than average growth.

First Pass WTG: Closed Comb Side
Fairly smooth, reasonably efficient. Not good enough for a one pass shave.

Second Pass XTG: Closed Com Side
Not especially smooth and after the pass I wasn't especially happy with the hair removal. It still felt like there was a lot of hair, but I press on! Still not good enough even for everyday.

Third Pass ATG: Open Comb Side
Quite scratchy. I could feel the blade and I didn't enjoy the pass much. A lost of hair remained. Quite disappointing. Not a good shave at all.

Buff up and J-hooking
I would have had to spend an age buffing and cleaning up because the beard was still quite rough, and frankly my face already felt quite irritated. So I did not buff with the Acevivi. There are limits to my testing zeal!

Finishing the Shave:
Sometimes a specific blade is no good. In the interests of fairness, I took the blade out and put it in my most efficient razor, the RR Wunderbar slant (which incidentally is not a blade this razor likes a lot) and had to shave again, because the shave with the Acevivi was just not good enough even for everyday.

I then did another ATG pass, and boy, was there a lot to remove! The jawline and neck still had a lot of growth. The blade performed well in the Wunderbar, so I don't think one can't blame the blade.

Now I'm not comparing a $13 razor to a $130. That would be grossly unfair. I just wanted to see if the blade was to blame, and frankly I didn't want to irritate my face more than necessary.

I did a clean up and buffing pass, using the Wunderbar and got a BBS shave. However the feedback from alum block revealed a lot of irritation, not surprising given three indifferent and inefficient passes with the Acevivi, followed by another ATG pass and cleanup with the Wunderbar, though I don't blame the latter. No nicks, cuts, or weepers, but definite neck irritation.

Conclusion:
The razor is well made, and low in price, and I admire the innovation, but I cannot recommend the Acevivi, unless you want to get a very long handle that's well made for under $13. It's possible that it will do better with other blades, but my initial impression is that it's not really worth testing. It's a fairly mild and quite inefficient razor.

Is it good value? No! The Ming Shi 2000s Adjustable is cheaper (aroud $10 from China) and better in every possible way. The following razors, all under $20 are much, much better: The RazoRock (RR) German 37 Slant, the Phoenix Artisan Accoutrements Bakelite slant, the RR Slab, the RR Mission, the RR Old Type, the RR Jaws (if you can get it), and probably some others too.

It's a pretty poor razor, and even at the low price not worth it. I wouldn't recommend it to newbies, because it will put them off DE shaving. Here's one to pass. [1.0/5] One Star Fell apart after a month of use. Cheap quality. [1.0/5] One Star Hubby didn't care for it. AND HE WANTED IT. [2.0/5] didn't receive everything offered! I got the razor but not the stand!! Also the head of the razor is plastic and didn't grip the blade tight. Luckily my old gillette razor head fit on this handle so I got the long handle I wanted. [5.0/5] Classy, durable and elegant! Great as a gift! [[VIDEOID:8b9fe3ccc3d2cdcd02b52d60c9c869fb]] I just bought this double edge handled safety razor set of ACEVIVI and I’m so glad that I did! I really like it as it features durability and of high quality. It is chrome plated, has nice grip and it does not slip while working on my facial hair according to my husband. I like that it has long handles, thick and safe. It is ideal for people with large hands! It is very sharp and works well on removing the facial hair of my husband.

What I like most about this razor is that it is of high quality. The construction is really great with very impressive details! Also, it has some weight on it. Thanks to the aluminum used, solid and very durable!

I gave it to him as a birthday gift and he was so pleased with it! It was his first time to own such classy and elegant looking razor with 5 blades! It came with a nice box so I just wrap it with a birthday wrapper. I highly recommend it!

I was blessed to receive this product at a discounted price in exchange for an honest/unbiased review during its promotional period. This is my personal experience and I wasn’t obliged to give positive reviews. I will update my review as soon as I encounter some problems with the product. [2.0/5] Rust Used for about a month. 2-3 times a week. Went to change the razor but it wouldn't untwist. The threads rusted together. Ended up twisting the stud off so no more razor. Good performance up to that point. [1.0/5] who recently started using a safety razor like me because the blades are so much cheaper Despite the box saying this razor was "for gentlemen" I bought it for my wife, who recently started using a safety razor like me because the blades are so much cheaper. We were also intrigued by the head being open comb on one side, closed on the other. Unfortunately, the very first time we unscrewed the head, the peg broke! The head is extremely cheap and shoddy, which is ironic because the handle is very thick, heavy, and hefty. When I attempted to return the item, I was informed that it wasn't eligible for return or refund. Fortunately, when I contacted Amazon via live chat, they refunded the price of the item. I've attached pictures of the broken item. The little tiny bit is the broken-off peg. We did NOT drop this or anything. It just broke unscrewing the head. [5.0/5] Great price. Heavy metal Very close shave! Great price. Heavy metal. [2.0/5] The handle is heavy and has good knurling however it rusts in the threads The handle is heavy and has good knurling however it rusts in the threads. Did not appreciate lame excuse seller gave when I reported the issue. The head is garbage and made of plastic coated by metal paint. I give it two stars because the handle is usable but low qualilty. [5.0/5] Five Stars great as a gift, very nice blade and good quality. it is obvious it will last a long time [5.0/5] This razor is not sharp which is good since I tend to cut myself by accident as ... This razor is not sharp which is good since I tend to cut myself by accident as I am a clumsy person.
It is also easy to use [1.0/5] *Update* don’t buy UPDATE: tried replacing the blade for the first time. as I don’t shave very often, it’s been a few months since trying to open the blade holder. The screw that holds the unit together break clean in half. DONT BUY THIS.

Pretty good unit. The only issue I had with this product was the installation of the razor was not clever as more expensive units. Removal of the handle was a bit rough and not designed like some more expensive safety razor. For the price you can't beat the quality of material. The razor handle felt hefty and the knurling has a good feel in the hand. [5.0/5] recommend it I bought this for my husband shaving acevivi and liked it a lot . It has 5 starter blades. It has very good quality. Color chrome plated looks very elegant and the price is very affordable [5.0/5] A hidden treasure on Amazon When I received the ACEVIVI razor I was blown away by its quality and craftsmanship. The checkered handle and rust proof aluminum head assembly was an added bonus. This is a top notch razor that will compliment a good blade and shaving soap or creme. No pressure needed because the razor is heavy. It glides over my whiskers with little effort. I'm very happy.
Thank You Amazon!!

response:
Many buyer like razor. Handle heavy, good grip, long - good for big hands. Chrome look nice, come in fancy box. Some say best shave ever, beat expensive razors. Good gift.

But many problem too. Thread rust together, razor break when unscrew head. Peg snap off first use. Head made of cheap plastic under metal paint. Some say razor not shave well, leave much hair behind.

Price low, under $13. Some say good deal, some say better razors exist for same money. Buyer beware - quality not consistent. Some razor last, some fall apart fast.
